data.sentence: Alright, I think we've got everyone, let me just — yeah, okay, Diana's here, Megan's here, Nina just joined. Good. Let's get started.
Sorry I'm a minute late, I was wrapping up something with the support team.
No worries. So, February wrap — I want to keep this tight, maybe twenty minutes. Megan, you want to kick us off on the product side?

Yeah, sure. So February was actually a pretty solid month for us on the product front. The big thing obviously is Comply v2 — we are tracking toward the April GA, the engineering team hit all their February milestones, which honestly I was a little worried about going into the month.
What were the milestones specifically? Just so we're all aligned.

So the two big ones were — finishing the multi-framework reporting backend, so that's the SOC 2, PCI DSS, HIPAA, ISO 27001 support — all of that is done on the backend side. And then the second was the on-demand report generation pipeline, which went through its first full integration pass.
Okay, and Nina, from the QA side — where are we on testing coverage for Comply v2?

So we're at about sixty-two percent coverage right now on the new modules. We had planned to be closer to seventy by end of February, so we're a little behind. The on-demand pipeline took longer to instrument than we expected — there were some async job handling edge cases that needed more test scaffolding.
Eight points behind — is that recoverable by, what's the target before GA, eighty-five percent?
Eighty percent is the target. And yeah, I think it's recoverable but it does depend on whether we get the two additional QA resources that were supposed to come over from the Detect team in March.
That's still the plan. Marcus and — is it Priya? — they're finishing up their current sprint on Detect and then they're supposed to rotate over. I don't see that changing.
Okay, good. I just wanted to flag it so it's on the record, not trying to raise a red flag or anything.
No, it's good to flag. That's what these calls are for. Diana, what's the customer success angle on February — anything notable?
Yeah, so a couple things. Renewals for the month came in at ninety-one percent, which is in line with our Q1 target. We did lose one account — Harrington Financial, they went with a competitor.
Do we know who they went with?

VaultEdge. They were pretty upfront about it — their procurement team said VaultEdge came in lower on price and had a compliance module that was, quote, more mature. Which, I mean, that stings a little given what we're about to launch.
Timing. That's just timing. If Harrington had waited another six weeks they'd have seen Comply v2 and that conversation might've gone differently.

I know, I know. I'm not blaming anyone, it's just frustrating. The good news is we've already got two upsell conversations going — one with Brentwood Logistics on adding Identity to their Protect subscription, and another with TechCore on expanding their Detect seat count.
What's the ARR on those two if they close?
Combined, roughly two-forty ARR. Brentwood is the bigger one — they're a mid-market account but they've got a pretty complex identity environment so we're not underselling.
Okay. Megan, on the Detect side — anything from the engineering team I should know about going into March? I know there's been some chatter about the event processing pipeline.

Yeah, so — and this is something I was going to bring up separately but since we're here — the infrastructure team flagged a potential bottleneck in the ingestion layer. It's not critical right now but it's something they want to address in March before it becomes a problem.
How long has that been on the radar?
Honestly, a few weeks. It got flagged during a load test in late January and it's been sitting in the backlog. I think it should probably get elevated.
Yeah, let's — let's not let that sit. Can you get me a write-up by Tuesday? I want to understand the scope before we assign resources to it.
Will do.
Nina, anything from QA on the Detect side? Since you're embedded across both products right now.

Nothing that rises to the level of a flag, really. Regression suite has been stable. The one thing I'd say is we've had a few intermittent test failures on the event processing tests specifically — like, they pass on rerun, which makes them hard to track down. Could be related to what Megan's describing, could be test environment flakiness, we're not sure yet.
Okay, that's worth noting. Loop in the infrastructure team on those test failures — even if it's just for awareness.
Already on it, I sent them the logs yesterday.
Good. Alright, let's talk March priorities quickly. For product the focus is obviously Comply v2 — getting to code freeze by the third week of March so Nina's team has enough runway. Agreed, Megan?
Agreed. I do want to have a conversation about whether we shift any Identity roadmap items to Q3 to free up eng bandwidth — I don't want to have that debate on this call, but I flagged it.
Let's schedule that for next week — just you and me and maybe Lena from eng leadership. Diana, from your side, March priorities?

Close Brentwood and TechCore, obviously. And I want to get a Comply v2 early access briefing set up for at least three or four of our strategic accounts before the April launch — let them know it's coming, manage expectations, get some pre-launch testimonials if we can.
I like that. Megan, can you get Diana something she can use for those briefings — not a full deck, but talking points and maybe a draft feature summary?
Yeah, I can get that done by end of next week.
Perfect. Okay, I think that covers the main stuff. Anyone have anything else before we wrap?
Nothing from me.
I'm good. Thanks everyone.
Alright, good call. Let's have a strong March — a lot riding on the next few weeks. Talk soon.
